questions.question: According to AFH 1, when is change appropriate?
options: When leadership directs it regardless of circumstances
When there is a perceived gap between what the norms are and what they should be
When the majority of employees vote for it
When the organization has been static for more than one year

questions.question: What is the best quality a leader can demonstrate when coping with change?
What is an essential element for preventing or overcoming resistance to change?
options: Offering financial incentives to all employees
Establishing a well thought out plan
Replacing employees who resist the change
Delaying the change until all resistance is eliminated

questions.question: Which common response to change occurs when organizational structure is so rigid that it inhibits change and possibly damages professional relationships?
What is the role of the 'change agent' in organizational change?
options: The person who resists organizational change
The person advocating for or leading the organizational change
The outside consultant who evaluates the change after implementation
The person responsible for documenting the change in official records

questions.question: Which approach to reducing resistance to change can negatively affect attitudes and potentially cause long-term negative consequences?
Who proposed the three-stage change process model of unfreezing, changing, and refreezing?
What is the most neglected, yet essential part of the unfreezing stage?
options: Creating an environment where people feel the need for change
Identifying the change agent for the project

questions.question: During Stage 2 (Changing) of the three-stage change process, what does the changing stage involve modifying?
options: Only personnel assignments and duty titles
Technology, tasks, structure, or distribution of people

questions.question: During the refreezing stage, what is a critical step?
options: Immediately beginning a new change initiative
Returning to the old process if any issues arise
Remaining engaged and evaluating results to determine if the change reached the desired effect

questions.question: What does Continuous Improvement (CI) increase while reducing associated costs?
Which CI methodology is focused on workflow, customer value, and eliminating process waste, with a primary focus on eliminating non-value-added activities?
Which CI methodology is a rigorous, data-driven approach focused on minimizing waste through identifying, controlling, and reducing process variation?
How many steps are in the Practical Problem-Solving Method?
In the Practical Problem-Solving Method, what should a problem statement NOT assume?
options: The scope and direction of the problem
A root cause, solution, or countermeasure
The who, what, when, and where of the problem

questions.question: According to the Practical Problem-Solving Method, what is the most commonly neglected step?
options: Step 1: Clarify and Validate the Problem

questions.question: What is the most common mistake made during Continuous Improvement efforts?
What are the three levels of effort available for the Practical Problem-Solving Method?
options: Just Do It, Rapid Improvement Event, and Improvement Project

questions.question: What are the three main constraints most often realized in project management?
What is a work breakdown structure?
options: A list of employees assigned to each phase of a project
A schedule showing the timeline for project completion
A technique based on dividing a project into sub-units or work packages
A risk assessment document identifying potential project failures

questions.question: Which common response to change occurs when people experience fear of the unknown or see the change as a threat to organizational stability and their job security?
Which common response to change occurs when people consider their current position or role and question the direction and capabilities of those in positions of power after the change?
Which approach to reducing resistance to change involves actively involving those affected in designing and implementing the change?
In the three-stage change process, what must leaders analyze to overcome resistance during unfreezing?
options: Restraining or opposing forces and devise ways to reduce them

questions.question: During Stage 2 (Changing), what may indicate that the change is being implemented too early?
During the refreezing stage, what helps the new behavior stick?
options: Actively encouraging and reinforcing the use of new techniques
Immediately beginning a new change initiative
Transferring employees who resist the change

questions.question: Which CI methodology is a comprehensive process requiring a change in the fundamental way business processes are performed and identifies unnecessary activities to eliminate them?
Which CI methodology is a systematic approach to optimize resource utilization by identifying, exploiting, subordinating, elevating, and reassessing constraints (bottlenecks) in the process?
In the Practical Problem-Solving Method, Step 1 (Clarify and Validate the Problem) incorporates which aspects of the Osborn-Parnes model?
options: Mess Finding, Data Finding, and Problem Finding
Idea Finding, Solution Finding, and Acceptance Finding

questions.question: In the Practical Problem-Solving Method, what does Step 4 (Determine Root Cause) involve?
options: Setting improvement targets based on strategic goals
Root cause analysis with the correct root cause validated by the same data used in Step 1
Developing countermeasures for potential root causes
Confirming results and verifying sustainability of improvements

questions.question: In the Practical Problem-Solving Method, Step 5 (Develop Countermeasures) incorporates which aspects of the Osborn-Parnes model?
options: Mess Finding, Data Finding, and Problem Finding
Idea Finding, Solution Finding, and Acceptance Finding

questions.question: According to the Practical Problem-Solving Method, what should happen if improvement targets are not met in Step 7?
options: It may be necessary to return to Step 4 (Determine Root Cause)
Close the project and document lessons learned
Restart the entire eight-step process from Step 1

questions.question: In Step 8 (Standardize Successful Processes), which of the following is a key question to consider?
options: How much budget was spent on the improvement project?
Who should be disciplined for the original process failures?
When should the next improvement project begin?
How should improvements and lessons learned be communicated?

questions.question: A 'Just Do It' (point improvement) approach to problem solving involves how many people and can be accomplished in what timeframe?
options: A large team and conducted over several weeks
A small team of subject matter experts and less than a week
One person (or a small team) and less than a day
An entire squadron and less than a month

questions.question: A Rapid Improvement Event consists of what size team and can be accomplished in what timeframe?
options: One person and less than a day
A small team of subject matter experts and less than a week
A large team and conducted over several months
An entire unit and less than two weeks

questions.question: According to AFH 1, how is project management defined?
options: The process of budgeting and allocating resources for a program
The act of delegating tasks to team members and tracking completion
The method of evaluating project outcomes against industry benchmarks
The process of leading, coordinating, planning, and controlling a diverse and complex set of processes and people in pursuit of achieving an established objective

questions.question: According to AFH 1, what is the difference between a project and a program?
options: A project is ongoing while a program is temporary
A project is temporary, based on an established objective, while programs are ongoing
A project requires more resources than a program
There is no difference; the terms are interchangeable

questions.question: In project management, the 'cost' constraint refers to what?
options: Only the monetary budget for the project
The price of procuring new equipment
Resource constraints including money, manpower, machinery, and materials
The financial cost of delaying the project

questions.question: What is the final step in the project management process?
options: Close the project by documenting lessons learned

questions.question: What information does a task analysis provide that is NOT depicted in a work breakdown structure?
options: The budget allocation for each work element
Task milestones, how the milestones can be measured, and resources or requirements
